reasoning:
The baseline value on 2025-11-09 is 3.87, coming off the late October 2025 Fed rate cut. 
1) 2025-11-16 (Short horizon): EFFR frequently remains flat or bounces down slightly within its target range, as seen in the first week of November. The chance of a 1bp upward drift in ~one week is relatively small (p=0.16).
2) 2025-12-09 (Medium horizon): This date immediately precedes the next FOMC decision on Dec 10, meaning the target band will be unchanged. With an extra month, the probability of an upward micro-drift increases, similar to the 4.09 -> 4.12 movement observed between September and late October (p=0.27).
3) 2026-02-07 (Long horizon): By February, the Fed will have made decisions in December and January. While markets currently price in a ~68% chance of further cuts (which would drop the rate well below 3.87), there is rising uncertainty regarding inflationary pressure and the potential for a Fed pause or hawkish pivot. Following the instruction that uncertainty should INCREASE with horizon, the probability for the most distant date is moved closest to 0.5 (p=0.40).
